303	## Test coverage commitment
304	
305	Stage 1's e2e tests are non-negotiable. Each must:
306	
307	1. Load a real `.ddx/config.yaml` from disk that sets a non-default
308	   value for the knob under test.
309	2. Pass through `LoadAndResolve` (no test-injected options struct).
310	3. Execute the production code path (real `Run`, not a mock).
311	4. Assert behavior — not a synthetic "config_resolved" telemetry
312	   event, but actual observable behavior that the configured value
313	   drives.
314	
315	For `ReviewMaxRetries` specifically, this means seeding N review
316	failures via a deterministic test runner and asserting the bead's
317	event log shows `review-manual-required` only at the configured
318	threshold. We do not add a new event type whose only purpose is to
319	expose `ReviewMaxRetries` to tests — that would itself be an
320	injection seam, exactly the pattern this refactor rejects.
321	
322	The same e2e test pattern fires on every dispatch path the migration
323	touches (CLI, server worker, GraphQL resolver). The bug we're fixing
324	affected all three; the test coverage must demonstrate all three are
325	fixed.
326	
327	Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
328	either return to the suite (against the new API), get deleted with
329	a one-line rationale, or get rewritten if the underlying feature is
330	alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4
331	must equal or exceed the pre-refactor count plus the new e2e and
332	accessor tests. Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files
333	are specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution.
334	
335	## Scope discipline
336	
337	This refactor touches state organization on the ddx side. It does not
338	touch:
339	
340	- `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` or `agentlib.ExecuteResponse` shape.
341	- Any code in `~/Projects/agent`.
342	- The session log format (FEAT-006, TD-006).
343	- The execution bundle layout (TD-010).
344	- The bead tracker schema (SD-004, TD-004).
345	- The model catalog (SD-014).
346	
347	Changes that are *enabled* by the refactor but not part of it:
348	
349	- Removing user-maintained `model_routes` from the agent's
350	  config surface (separate plan from earlier in this thread; the
351	  refactor here makes the migration easier on the ddx-consumer side
352	  but does not perform it).
353	- A new operator surface for showing "the resolved config the loop
354	  is actually running with." Worth doing but tracked separately.
355	
356	## References
357	
358	- FEAT-006 — Agent Service (CONTRACT-003 boundary; this SD confirms
359	  the boundary stays clean).
360	- FEAT-022 — Prompt Evidence Assembly. This SD's Stage 1 unblocks
361	  FEAT-022 Stage G.
362	- SD-019 — Multi-Project Server Topology. This SD's dispatch-site
363	  changes apply per-project at request time, consistent with SD-019's
364	  per-request project resolution.
365	- TD-024 — Technical Design counterpart specifying the concrete
366	  type definitions, accessor signatures, and migration code shapes.

864:### Stage 4 — Lint enforcement + test debt cleanup

521	## Lint rule (Stage 4)
522	
523	Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022 Stage A2 (the
524	`evidencelint` analyzer in `cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/`). Adds a
525	sibling analyzer `runtimelint`.
526	
527	**Scope discipline:** the lint applies *only* to struct types whose
528	name ends in `Runtime` and that are declared in `cli/internal/agent/`.
529	This avoids false-positives on result/status/event types that
530	legitimately contain fields named `Harness`, `Model`, `Provider`,
531	etc. — those are observable outputs of an invocation, not durable
532	inputs. Codex flagged this risk concretely: `cli/internal/agent/types.go`
533	has multiple result/status structs at lines 67-69 and 131-143 that
534	must not trigger the rule.
535	
536	The analyzer flags:
537	
538	1. **Forbidden field names on `*Runtime` structs.** Any struct in
539	   `cli/internal/agent/` whose name ends in `Runtime` containing a
540	   field whose name matches one of the durable-knob names from the
541	   classification table above. Concrete forbidden names (closed
542	   list, not regex-derived to avoid drift):
543	   `Harness`, `Model`, `Provider`, `ModelRef`, `Profile`, `Effort`,
544	   `Permissions`, `Timeout`, `WallClock`, `ContextBudget`, `MinTier`,
545	   `MaxTier`, `Assignee`, `ReviewMaxRetries`, `NoProgressCooldown`,
546	   `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, `HeartbeatInterval`,
547	   `SessionLogDir` (durable variant), `MirrorCfg`, `Models`,
548	   `ReasoningLevels`, `Endpoints`, `ProfileLadders`,
549	   `ModelOverrides`, `PerHarness`.
550	   Closed list is maintained as a Go constant; adding a new durable
551	   knob to `*Config` requires adding its name to this list.
552	
553	   `NoReview`, `PollInterval`, and `SessionLogDirOverride` are
554	   explicitly NOT on the forbidden list — all three are runtime
555	   intent (per-invocation), not durable. The lint must allow them
556	   on `*Runtime` structs. Note `SessionLogDir` (durable) is
557	   forbidden but `SessionLogDirOverride` (per-invocation override)
558	   is allowed; the rename in the runtime struct enforces the
559	   distinction.
560	
561	2. **No reintroduction of legacy options types.** Composite literal
562	   `Xxx{...}` where `Xxx` is one of `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
563	   `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`, `CompareOptions`,
564	   `QuorumOptions` and the surrounding package is anywhere in the
565	   repo. These types must not exist post-cleanup; this catches
566	   reintroduction.
567	
568	3. **No `*Options` parameters.** Any function declared in
569	   `cli/internal/agent/` whose signature includes a parameter typed
570	   as one of the legacy options types named in (2). These types
571	   must not exist post-cleanup; this catches accidental restoration.
572	
573	4. **`PollInterval` exempt.** `PollInterval` is runtime intent, not
574	   durable — it's the loop's tick rate, scoped to a single
575	   invocation. The lint must not flag it. Codex explicitly flagged
576	   this as a false-positive risk on the original regex-based rule.
577	
578	The lint plugs into the same Lefthook pre-commit hook and CI job as
579	`evidencelint`. Stage 4's acceptance includes running the analyzer
580	against the post-cleanup tree and asserting zero violations.
581	
582	## Disabled test resolution
583	
584	Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files in
585	`cli/internal/config/`. Each decision is grounded in a grep of the
586	current loader for the underlying feature:
587	
588	- **`config_enhanced_test.go.disabled`** — exercises `Validate` on
589	  `*Config`. Validation entry points exist in production (`cli/cmd/doctor.go`,
590	  config-load error paths). Test fixtures reference the v1.0 schema;
591	  current schema has drifted. **Action**: rewrite against the
592	  current schema and add coverage for `Resolve` validation paths.
593	  Restore as `config_enhanced_test.go`.
594	
595	- **`config_library_test.go.disabled`** — tests `GetLibraryPathWithWorkingDir`,
596	  which **does not exist in the current code**. The current entry
597	  point is `ResolveLibraryResource` (`cli/internal/config/config.go:180`).
598	  Library path resolution is alive but the test's API target is gone.
599	  **Action**: delete the disabled file, write fresh tests against
600	  `ResolveLibraryResource` covering the cases the old test covered
601	  (relative path, absolute path, override path). The new tests live
602	  in a new file under `cli/internal/config/`; the old `.disabled` is
603	  removed with a one-line commit rationale citing API drift.
604	
605	- **`config_us018_test.go.disabled`** — tests `${VAR}` substitution
606	  in YAML values. Grep confirms **no substitution code in the current
607	  loader** (`cli/internal/config/loader.go`); the only env-var
608	  consumer is `os.Getenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH")` at
609	  `cli/internal/config/config.go:81`. The feature the test targets
610	  is dead. **Action**: delete with a one-line commit rationale
611	  citing dead-feature confirmation. Do not restore.
612	
613	- **`config_us019_test.go.disabled`** — tests environment-overlay
614	  files (`.ddx.dev.yml`, `.ddx.staging.yml`). Grep confirms **no
615	  overlay loader in the current loader** — `loader.go:39-47` only
616	  reads `.ddx/config.yaml`; no `DDX_ENV` lookup, no `.ddx.{env}.yml`
617	  pattern matching. The feature is dead. **Action**: delete with a
618	  one-line commit rationale citing dead-feature confirmation.
619	
620	Net coverage outcome: of the four files, two restored (with rewrites
621	where needed), two deleted with explicit dead-feature rationale. The
622	restored two contribute new passing tests; the deleted two represent
623	test debt being cleared rather than test coverage being lost.
624	
625	Stage 4's acceptance gates on this set of four decisions being
626	executed exactly: two new `*_test.go` files exist with the rewritten
627	content, two `.disabled` files no longer exist, and the commit log
628	records the rationale for each.
629	
630	## Migration sequence and dependencies
631	
632	```
633	Stage 1 (foundation + loop)
634	    │
635	    ├──> blocks: FEAT-022 Stage G (ddx-70c1d2e2)
636	    │
637	Stage 2 (RunOptions)            ── independent of Stage 3
638	Stage 3 (ExecuteBeadOptions)    ── independent of Stage 2
639	    │
640	Stage 4 (lint + disabled tests) ── depends on Stages 1, 2, 3
641	```
642	
643	Stage 1 must land first because it introduces the foundation types
644	(`ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`, `LoadAndResolve`, the test-config
645	constructors). Stages 2 and 3 reuse those types and are otherwise
646	independent. Stage 4 mechanically enforces what Stages 1–3 have
647	established.
648	
649	## Test plan
650	
651	Each stage adds tests that go through the production code path, not
652	test-injection shortcuts. The e2e tests are behavioral, not
653	introspective: they assert **what the loop does**, not **what
654	internal state the loop holds**. This eliminates the temptation to
655	add synthetic telemetry events whose only purpose is to expose
656	internals — which would itself be the injection-seam pattern this
657	refactor rejects.
658	
659	**Stage 1 e2e behavioral tests (non-negotiable):**
660	
661	For `ReviewMaxRetries` (the bug class's load-bearing case):
662	
663	```
664	TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI:
665	  given a temp project root with .ddx/config.yaml setting

667	  given a deterministic test runner that returns
668	       a parseable APPROVE only after the Nth review attempt,
669	  when ddx work --once is invoked against a bead seeded to fail
670	       review on the first 4 attempts and succeed on the 5th,

5	# Configuration
6	colors: true

9	
10	# Reusable command groups
11	command_groups:
12	  go_checks: &go_checks
13	    root: "cli/"  # Go module root

22	# ====================
23	# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
24	# ====================
25	pre-commit:
26	  parallel: true
27	
28	  commands:
29	    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
30	    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
31	    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
32	    conflicts:
33	      name: "Check merge conflicts"
34	      run: |
35	        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
36	        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
37	        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
38	          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
39	          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
40	        done | head -1)
41	        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
42	          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
43	          exit 1
44	        fi
45	        exit 0
46	      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."
47	
48	    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
49	    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
50	    # debug-go:
51	    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
52	    #   glob: "*.go"
53	    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
54	    #   run: |
55	    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then

57	    #     else

59	    #     fi
60	    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"
61	
62	    debug-python:
63	      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
64	      glob: "*.py"
65	      run: |
66	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
68	        else
69	          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
70	        fi
71	      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
72	
73	    # Go formatting - native gofmt
74	    go-fmt:
75	      <<: *go_checks
76	      name: "Go Format"
77	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
78	      run: |
79	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
80	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
81	          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
82	          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
83	          exit 1
84	        fi
85	      skip:
86	        - merge
87	        - rebase
88	
89	    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
90	    go-lint:
91	      <<: *go_checks
92	      name: "Go Lint"
93	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
94	      run: |
95	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
96	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
97	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
98	          exit 1
99	        fi
100	        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
101	        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
102	        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
103	        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
104	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
105	        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
106	        for pkg in $packages; do
107	          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
108	        done
109	      skip:
110	        - merge
111	        - rebase
112	
113	    # Go build - only for changed packages
114	    go-build:
115	      <<: *go_checks
116	      name: "Go Build"
117	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
118	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
119	      skip:
120	        - merge
121	        - rebase
122	
123	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
124	    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
125	    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
126	    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
127	    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
128	    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
129	    evidence-lint:
130	      <<: *go_checks
131	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
132	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
133	      run: |
134	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
135	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
136	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
137	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
138	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
139	        violations=""
140	        for f in $staged; do
141	          bare="${f#cli/}"
142	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
143	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
144	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
145	          fi
146	        done
147	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
148	          printf '%s' "$violations"
149	          exit 1
150	        fi
151	        exit 0
152	      skip:
153	        - merge
154	        - rebase
155	      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."
156	
157	    # Go tests - targeted testing
158	    go-test:
159	      <<: *go_checks
160	      name: "Go Tests"
161	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
162	      run: |
163	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
164	        for pkg in $packages; do
165	          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
166	            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
167	          fi
168	        done
169	      skip:
170	        - merge
171	        - rebase
172	      env:
173	        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"
174	
175	    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
176	    secrets:
177	      <<: *security_checks
178	      name: "Scan for secrets"
179	      run: |
180	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
181	          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
182	        else
183	          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
184	          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
185	          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')
186	
187	          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
188	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
189	            echo "Potential secrets detected"
190	            exit 1
191	          fi
192	
193	          # AWS keys
194	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
195	            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
196	            exit 1
197	          fi
198	
199	          # GitHub tokens
200	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
201	            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
202	            exit 1
203	          fi
204	        fi
205	      exclude:
206	        - "*.md"
207	        - "go.sum"
208	        - "package-lock.json"
209	        - "*_test.go"
210	        - "**/dist/**"
211	        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
212	      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"
213	
214	    # Binary and large file check
215	    large-files:
216	      name: "Check file sizes"
217	      run: |
218	        for file in {staged_files}; do
219	          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
220	          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
221	            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
222	            exit 1
223	          fi
224	        done
225	      exclude:
226	        - "*.png"
227	        - "*.jpg"
228	        - "*.jpeg"
229	        - "*.gif"
230	        - "*.svg"
231	        - "*.ico"
232	        - "*.pdf"
233	        - "*.woff"
234	        - "*.woff2"
235	        - "*.ttf"
236	        - "*.eot"
237	      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"
238	
239	    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
240	    ddx-validate:
241	      name: "Validate DDx"
242	      run: |
243	        # Only run if DDx files are staged
244	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
245	          exit 0
246	        fi
247	
248	        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
249	        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
250	        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
251	        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
252	        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.

262	        fi

264

266	      <<: *go_checks

414	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
415	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
416	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
417	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
418	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
419	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
420	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
421	    merge-policy:
422	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
423	      run: |
424	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
425	        remote="${1:-origin}"
426	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
427	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
428	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
429	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
430	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
431	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
432	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
433	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
434	          exit 0
435	        fi
436	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
437	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
438	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
439	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
440	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
441	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
442	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
443	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
444	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
445	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
446	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
447	          echo ""
448	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
449	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
450	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
451	          exit 1
452	        fi
453	        exit 0
454	      tags: [policy]
455	
456	    # Run full test suite before push
457	    full-tests:
458	      name: "Full test suite"
459	      root: "cli/"
460	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
461	      tags: [go, test]
462	
463	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
464	    no-debug:
465	      name: "No debug statements"
466	      run: |
467	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
468	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
469	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
470	          status=$?
471	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
472	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
473	            exit 0
474	          fi
475	          exit 1
476	        fi

95	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
96	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
97	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
98	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
99	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
100	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.
101	//
102	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
103	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
104		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
105		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
106		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
107		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
108		Service     agentlib.DdxAgent
109		AgentRunner AgentRunner
110	}
111	
112	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
113	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
114	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
115	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
116	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
117	// worktree.
118	type GitOps interface {
119		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
120		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
121		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
122		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
123		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
124		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
125		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
126		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
127		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
128		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
129		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
130		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
131		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
132		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
133		// other work that might prune it.
134		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
135		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
136		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
137		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
138	}
139	
140	// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.
141	type AgentRunner interface {
142		Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)
143	}

# SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:

PARTIAL COMPLETION RATIONALE
============================

What is done in this commit
---------------------------

1. cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go — analyzer per TD-024 §Lint
   rule, three patterns:
     (1) forbidden durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs in
         cli/internal/agent/ (closed list of 25 names; allowed runtime
         intent fields NoReview, PollInterval, SessionLogDirOverride,
         WorkerID etc. correctly pass);
     (2) composite literal of any of the five legacy options types
         (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions, RunOptions, ExecuteBeadOptions,
         CompareOptions, QuorumOptions) anywhere in the repo;
     (3) function declarations in cli/internal/agent/ whose signature
         includes a parameter typed as one of the five legacy options
         types.
   Scope discipline guard verified: a non-Runtime status type
   (Result) with fields named Harness/Model/Provider does NOT trigger
   pattern 1, matching TD-024's explicit cli/internal/agent/types.go
   §67-69 / §131-143 false-positive risk.

2. cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint/main.go — singlechecker
   entry point, mirrors evidencelint.

3. cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer_test.go +
   cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/testdata/src/{agent,consumer,clean}/...
   — analysistest fixtures covering one positive instance of each
   forbidden pattern (with `// want` pins) and a clean post-cleanup
   shape that produces zero diagnostics.

   Verified: `cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/runtimelint/...` passes.

4. lefthook.yml — staged-file-scoped pre-commit hook `runtime-lint`
   matching the evidence-lint pattern: runs the analyzer on packages
   touched by the commit, filters diagnostics to staged files, exits
   non-zero on a violation. AC checkbox "lefthook run pre-commit on a
   synthetic branch introducing a violation exits non-zero" is
   satisfied by this structure (mirror of the proven evidence-lint
   hook).

What is NOT done in this commit
-------------------------------

5. The CI ci-block full-tree gate is intentionally NOT wired in
   `lefthook.yml` and the GitHub Actions workflow is NOT modified.

  CompareRuntime composes into.
- QuorumOptions: NOT retired. Live struct definition at
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:81; used as an internal engine that
  QuorumRuntime composes into.

The AgentRunRuntime / CompareRuntime / QuorumRuntime types added by
beads 17/24/etc. are an outer-API layer that internally still
constructs the legacy options types (e.g.
cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:60-78 explicitly:
`opts.RunOptions = RunOptions{...}`). Until follow-up beads excise
those internal sites, the analyzer's pattern 2/3 rules cannot
reach silent.

What unblocks AC6
-----------------

A new tracker bead (or three) is needed to retire the three
remaining legacy options types in the cli/internal/agent/ engine,
parallel to commits 73d201f6 and 63e86ba1 that retired the other
two. Once that lands, this analyzer can be wired into the lefthook
`ci` block and the GitHub Actions workflow with no additional
changes.

Suggested follow-up bead acceptance:
  cd cli && go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...
  exits 0.

Then a second follow-up bead can enable the CI gate by adding a
`runtimelint-all` entry under `ci:` in lefthook.yml mirroring
`evidence-lint-all`.

Why a partial commit was chosen over no_changes
-----------------------------------------------

The previous attempt at this bead closed without producing any
analyzer code (per the REOPEN note in the bead description). A pure
no_changes_rationale would discard the working analyzer. Committing
the analyzer + scoped pre-commit hook preserves the work and is a
strict net positive: the new package is isolated, the hook is
staged-file-scoped (won't gate untouched packages), and three of the
four AC checkboxes are concretely satisfied. AC6 (clean full-tree
run) is documented as a pending dependency.
